datasets.description: Item-level Marg sales invoice lines (pure commercial invoices only; excludes sales returns, challans, and accounting adjustments — matches the Sales Analysis dashboard)
Invoice-level Marg sales rollup (pure commercial invoices only; excludes sales returns, challans, and accounting adjustments — matches the Sales Analysis dashboard)
Item-level Marg purchase invoice lines (pure commercial invoices only; excludes purchase returns, challans, and accounting adjustments — matches the Purchase Analysis dashboard)
Invoice-level Marg purchase rollup (pure commercial invoices only; excludes purchase returns, challans, and accounting adjustments — matches the Purchase Analysis dashboard)
Current product-location stock and replenishment dataset
Batch-level stock, expiry, FEFO, and ageing dataset
Unified sales and purchase tax line register
Core GL journal lines and Marg party ledger postings

Item-level Marg sales returns — credit/debit notes and breakage-expiry, shown as POSITIVE magnitudes. Equals the Sales Analysis dashboard with scope=return. Does NOT net against invoices; for net-of-returns use the dashboard net scope.

Item-level Marg purchase returns — credit/debit notes and breakage-expiry, shown as POSITIVE magnitudes. Equals the Purchase Analysis dashboard with scope=return. Does NOT net against invoices; for net-of-returns use the dashboard net scope.
Item-level NET sales (invoices minus returns). net_amount is signed: invoices positive, returns negative. SUM(net_amount) equals the Sales Analysis dashboard scope=net.
Item-level NET purchases (invoices minus returns). net_amount signed: invoices positive, returns negative. Equals the Purchase Analysis dashboard scope=net.
Invoice-level Marg sales returns (one row per credit note / breakage-expiry receipt). Positive magnitudes. Use for return document counts and per-document values.
Invoice-level Marg purchase returns (one row per debit note / breakage-expiry return). Positive magnitudes.

Per-product item velocity from the Marg item master: last sold date, days since last sold, never-sold flag, movement status (NEVER_SOLD/NON_MOVING/SLOW_MOVING/MOVING), lifetime sold quantity, and current stock. The ONLY dataset that can list items that did NOT sell (it is the item master, not a transaction list). Use it for not-sold / never-sold / non-moving / dead-stock / slow-moving / idle / days-since-last-sold questions. Do NOT use a date range to express 'not sold in a period' - use the days_since_last_sold / never_sold filters instead.

businessRules: Uses sales item quantity from approved sales item view
Returns are included according to production status logic unless template filters exclude them

Line net equals absolute line amount plus absolute tax amount. Dataset is pure commercial invoices only (no returns/challans/adjustments), so this metric is gross-of-returns; it equals the Sales/Purchase Analysis dashboard headline total for the same scope.

metrics.expression: CASE WHEN SUM(SUM(quantity)) OVER () > 0 THEN (SUM(quantity) / SUM(SUM(quantity)) OVER () * 100)::float8 ELSE 0 END
businessRules: For top sold items, conversion percentage means the product's percentage contribution to total sold quantity in the filtered result set, matching existing item contribution report semantics
Gross amount equals ABS(quantity) multiplied by rate
Discount is gross amount less absolute line amount floored at zero
Taxable amount is line amount where tax rate is positive
Non-taxable amount is line amount where tax rate is zero
Tax amount uses ABS(gst_amount)
Cost fallback follows Marg stock purchase rate, last purchase rate, then product standard cost
Invoice-level dataset has one row per invoice
Counts distinct sales invoices from the item-level sales dataset so product filters are preserved
Invoice net uses the approved invoice final amount when present, otherwise summed line net amount
Gross amount is rolled up from item lines
Tax amount is rolled up from item lines
Uses purchase item quantity from approved purchase item view

Line net equals absolute line amount plus absolute tax amount. Dataset is pure commercial invoices only (no returns/challans/adjustments), so this metric is gross-of-returns; it equals the Sales/Purchase Analysis dashboard headline total for the same scope.
Tax amount uses ABS(gst_amount)
Invoice-level dataset has one row per purchase invoice
Invoice net uses the approved invoice final amount when present, otherwise summed line net amount
Tax amount is rolled up from purchase item lines
Uses the approved current on-hand stock quantity
Uses the approved available stock quantity
Uses the approved stock valuation amount
Use with filter stock_status IN BELOW_MINIMUM, BELOW_REORDER, NEGATIVE
Default dataset filter keeps reportable non-consumed/non-recalled non-zero batches
Use with expiry_date before current date
Use with an expiry date range to count distinct products with reportable stock batches expiring in that period
Use with an expiry date range to count reportable stock batches expiring in that period
Use with an expiry date range to value reportable stock batches expiring in that period
Uses signed inventory ledger quantity
Uses inventory ledger total cost
Use party_type = CUSTOMER and is_open = true
Use party_type = SUPPLIER and is_open = true
Default dataset filter keeps open invoices
Uses approved tax register derived from item views
PAN/VAT/GST identifiers require tax or detailed-report authorization
Trial balance must filter ledger_source = CORE_GL and status = POSTED
Trial balance must filter ledger_source = CORE_GL and status = POSTED
Core GL signed amount is debit minus credit; Marg party ledger signed amount follows Marg convention

defaultAssumptions.description: If no date range is provided, use the current financial year from the tenant default fiscal calendar when available.
defaultAssumptions.fallback: If fiscal calendar is unavailable, require the NLQ service to resolve the application's existing date default before execution.

defaultAssumptions.description: Cancelled invoices are excluded by default where a real cancellation/status field exists or is exposed by the view.
defaultAssumptions.implementation: Apply is_cancelled IS DISTINCT FROM true. The Marg sales/purchase views expose the real mv.is_cancelled value AND hard-exclude cancelled vouchers at the source (WHERE is_cancelled = FALSE), so cancelled documents never appear regardless of the default filter.

defaultAssumptions.description: Sales returns and purchase returns are not invented or netted differently by AI.
defaultAssumptions.implementation: The sales_items/sales_invoices/purchase_items/purchase_invoices datasets are PURE COMMERCIAL INVOICES ONLY — returns, challans, and accounting adjustments are excluded by construction (family = SALES_INVOICE / PURCHASE_INVOICE). status is always POSTED and document_type is always SALE/PURCHASE here. Returns are NOT available via these datasets; a dedicated returns dataset is planned. Never imply a returns figure from these datasets or net returns out of them.

defaultAssumptions.description: "Most selling product" and similar phrases mean highest sold quantity unless the user asks for value.
"Highest sales product" means highest net sales value unless the user asks for quantity.
"Party wise" means customer-wise in sales context, supplier-wise in purchase context, and party_type grouped outstanding in outstanding context.
"Item wise" means grouped by product/item using product code and product name.
For top sold item reports, "conversion percentage" means percentage contribution to total sold quantity unless the user explicitly asks for forecast, promotion, or UOM conversion.

synonyms: top items sold with conversion percentage
top items sold and contribution percentage

dashboardTemplates.description: Sales KPIs, trend, top products, top customers, salesman-wise sales, and tax summary
Purchase KPIs, top purchased products, supplier-wise purchase, and purchase tax summary
Current stock value, low stock, expiry risk, batch stock, and movement ledger summaries
Outstanding, trial balance, ledger, receivable, payable, and tax summary views

securityRules.description: AI can only query datasets listed in this catalog with allowedForNlq = true.
AI can only use approved metrics, dimensions, filters, and time fields from this catalog.
Every generated query must include tenant_id from the authenticated user context.
company_id filtering is mandatory for company-scoped users and datasets that expose company_id.
branch_id or warehouse_id filters must be intersected with user allowed branches/locations.
Existing JWT auth, roles, module gates, and report permissions must be checked before executing any NLQ report.
Raw SQL must not be exposed to normal users in prompts, responses, logs visible to users, or exports.
PAN/VAT/GST fields may be selected only for tax reports or authorized detailed reports.
Detail and ranking queries must include backend-enforced row limits.
Backend execution must use configured query timeout and cancellation handling.

disallowedOperations.description: Do not query Marg or ERP base tables directly from NLQ.
Do not allow INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, MERGE, TRUNCATE, ALTER, DROP, CREATE, GRANT, REVOKE, COPY, VACUUM, ANALYZE, REFRESH, or function execution from NLQ.
Do not execute model-generated SQL unless compiled from catalog-approved datasets, fields, filters, and templates by backend code.
Do not query without tenant_id or across tenants.
Do not bypass existing report module, role, company, branch, or financial permissions.
Do not run unbounded detail queries or exports without pagination and export authorization.
Do not include PAN/VAT/GST/customer/supplier legal identifiers unless required by the selected report template and authorized.
Do not invent cancellation, return, tax, net/gross, financial year, or branch logic outside approved views and report rules.
Do not expose database schema, source table names, indexes, or migration SQL to business users.
